name: Al Jolson
abstract: Al Jolson (born Asa Yoelson, Yiddish: אַסאַ יואלסאָן; c. May 26, 1886 (O.S.) June 9, 1886 (N.S.) – October 23, 1950) was an American singer, comedian, actor, and vaudevillian.

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: There's a show, funny enough, ironically, what's the most successful show in London right now? It's a show about Al Jolson. Yeah. Jolson of all things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: even Al Jolson, I mean he attempted to -- to get them in the pictures... but he was so happy, you know, he married he and Ruby Keeler

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Barbutti
about_quotes.evidence: You know Al Jolson was nuts when he said no such thing as a bad audience.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Barbutti interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 8, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Von Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: he had the Victrola right next to the piano, and I would wind up this Victrola so he could play his sounds, which of course was Fats Waller, Al Jolson, Rudy Vallee, and – let’s see – Louis Armstrong, of course. Those were his four favorites.
about_quotes.doc_title: Von Freeman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: Do you think Al Jolson could outsing me? In a pig's eye. ... Don't put her out of the show 'cause Al Jolson backed up and got in the show so why can't she brown down and be in this show.

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I would venture a guess that Al Jolson really came out of a minstrel show... to me, he reminded me of those blackface comedians that I saw as a kid.
